name: CRUD/basic
description: Basic CRUD conformance tests: create, read, update, delete lifecycle for a Patient resource.

variable.name: patientId
variable.description: The server-assigned ID from the create response

operation.description: Create a synthetic Patient to obtain a server-assigned ID for subsequent tests

test.name: create returns 201 and assigns an id
test.description: Server must respond with 201 Created and assign a non-empty id to the resource

assert.description: Setup create must have returned HTTP 201
Response body must be a Patient resource
Server must have assigned an id

test.name: read returns the created resource
test.description: GET /Patient/{id} must return the Patient that was just created

operation.description: Read the Patient by server-assigned ID

assert.description: Response body must be a Patient
Returned id must match the one assigned at create

test.name: update changes the resource
test.description: PUT /Patient/{id} must be accepted (2xx). Body field assertions require dynamic body construction which is not supported by this engine yet.

operation.description: Fetch current resource to use as update body
PUT the resource back (round-trip update). Field-level mutation requires dynamic body construction not yet supported.

assert.description: Update must return a success status (2xx)

test.name: read after update reflects the change
test.description: GET after PUT must return 200. Field-level change assertions require dynamic body construction not yet supported.

assert.description: Resource id must be stable across the update

test.name: delete removes the resource
test.description: DELETE /Patient/{id} must return 2xx (200, 202, or 204 are all valid per the FHIR spec)

assert.description: Delete must return a 2xx success status

test.name: read after delete returns 410 or 404
test.description: GET after DELETE must return 410 (Gone) or 404 (Not Found). FHIR R4 specifies 410 Gone for a resource that existed and was deleted, so that is the primary assertion. 404 is accepted as a warningOnly alternative for servers that do not distinguish deleted from never-existed.

operation.description: Attempt to read the deleted Patient

assert.description: Primary: expect 410 Gone (FHIR R4 behavior for a deleted resource)
Alternative: 404 Not Found is also accepted (warningOnly so 404-returning servers still pass this suite)

operation.description: Teardown: attempt to clean up the Patient (may already be deleted by the delete test — that is expected)
